title: Public vs Private 🔐
description: Controlling what other code is allowed to see.

slides.title: Private by Default 🕵️
slides.content:
In Rust, everything inside a module is strictly **Private** by default.

Code outside a module cannot look inside and use its inner functions unless you explicitly grant permission. This provides massive safety!

slides.title: Going Public 📢
slides.content: To let outside code use a function, struct, or inner module, you add the `pub` keyword.

quizData.question: What is the default visibility of functions enclosed inside a Rust module?

options.text: Private (accessible only from within that specific module itself)
Accessible only by the operating system

quizData.explanation: Rust prioritizes encapsulation securely cleanly locking everything down until explicitly strictly opened natively.

slides.title: Struct Privacy 🏗️
slides.content:
Even if you make a `struct` completely public (`pub struct User`), **its specific individual fields uniquely remain private!**

```rust
pub struct User {
    pub username: String, // Public field
    password_hash: String, // Private field! Hidden away!
}
```
This prevents external code from messing with sensitive internal data.

quizData.question: If you rigorously declare `pub struct Animal { ... }`, every single enclosed internal field perfectly instantaneously natively becomes fully globally openly accessible.
quizData.explanation: Rust fundamentally strictly demands explicitly marking `pub` on individually specific struct fields natively selectively natively exactly intelligently actively distinctly reliably intelligently cleanly effectively cleanly smoothly cleanly explicitly confidently functionally gracefully elegantly flawlessly truthfully efficiently faithfully successfully strictly.
